describes: Curated classification of every specification section that contains a capitalised MUST/MUST NOT, used by `tools/lint.py --coverage` to report normative coverage against the IMPL denominator instead of against every capitalised MUST.
why: Not every capitalised MUST is an implementation requirement a conformance runner could check. Counting all of them makes the coverage metric measure something other than what it claims. Curating the denominator is only honest if the curation is auditable, so every section below carries a one-line reason, and INCLUSION IS THE DEFAULT: a section is IMPL unless there is a stated reason a runner could not check it, and the reason must name what owns the requirement instead.

classes.IMPL: A constraint on a conformant implementation, owned by this clause. Needs a conformance case. This is the denominator the headline coverage number uses.
classes.ENCODING: A §18 wire-format field/CDDL table whose encoding is already exercised byte-exactly by a committed entry in conformance/vectors/. A §18 section with no vector behind it is IMPL, not ENCODING.
classes.RESTATEMENT: The requirement is real but owned by another clause — the §21 registry Action column, the §19 operations appendix (§10.4: the narrative governs), the §20 state-machine tables, or a summary/parity/limits table. A case should cite the owning clause, not the restatement.
classes.PROCEDURE: Binds an operator's process or a future registrant's document, not a wire behaviour: incident-response runbooks, the pre-deployment audit gate, IANA allocation and registration policies.
classes.NARRATIVE: The capitalised MUST is not a requirement at all — a list header ('DMTAP MUST provide:'), a counterfactual quoted in order to be rejected, a BCP 14 glossary entry, or a reference-table row.

audit: Regenerate the section list with `python3 tools/lint.py --coverage`; every section it reports must appear here exactly once. Disagreeing with a classification is a bug report: reclassify to IMPL and write the case.
limits: Coverage measured against this file is SECTION-level, not MUST-level: a section counts as covered if any case cites it, not if every MUST in it is exercised. It is a floor and deliberately generous.
It counts cases that exist, not cases that pass. Most of the suite is construction recipes and attestations; see the partition in conformance/SUITE.md. No implementation has been run against it.

The classification below is a judgement call. Inclusion is the default and every exclusion states its owner, but a reader who disagrees with one should reclassify it IMPL and write the case — that is the intended failure mode, and it moves the number.
